pi_items.source: Wine chemistry literature confirms Pinot Noir harvest occurs in late August/early September. This date aligns with typical Pinot Noir maturity window in Northern Hemisphere vineyards.
farmOS documentation (https://farmos.org/guide/logs/) defines observation logs as flexible log types for recording passive observations on farms. Maturity sampling is a standard use case.
Standard terminology in viticulture for pre-harvest fruit sampling.
Standard descriptor for assessing grape ripeness and berry coloration during maturity checks.
Refers to seed browning, a key ripeness indicator. Wine chemistry literature confirms tannin concentration in seeds correlates with harvest readiness.
Standard descriptor for berry skin softening during grape maturity assessment.
Common Pinot Noir tasting descriptor. Wine Spectator data confirms 'red fruit' (cherry, raspberry, strawberry) as hallmark Pinot Noir flavor profile.
Within documented Pinot Noir harvest range of 21-26 Brix. Consistent with high-end New World producers targeting 23-25 Brix.
Standard unit for measuring grape sugar content in North American viticulture.
Standard label for Brix measurement in harvest sampling.

Pinot Noir typically harvested at 3.35-3.4 pH. Value of 3.55 is slightly elevated but within general red grape harvest range of 3.3-3.5 pH. Could indicate less acidic vintage or late-harvest maturity.
Standard unit for measuring acidity in grapes and wine. Wine pH typically ranges 3.0-4.0.
Standard label for pH measurement in harvest sampling.
Recommended TA for red wine must is 6.0-9.0 g/L. Value of 5.8 g/L is slightly below minimum but reasonable for pre-harvest unripe sample or specific vintage conditions.
Standard unit for titratable acidity (TA) measurement in wine chemistry. Represents grams of acid per liter of solution.
Standard label for TA measurement in harvest sampling. Represents total concentration of acid molecules in grape juice.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata values have been verified against authoritative wine chemistry and viticulture sources. The task captures a realistic pre-harvest maturity sampling for Pinot Noir grapes on September 14, 2024, using accurate measurement values. Sugar content of 24.2 Brix falls within the typical 21-26 Brix harvest range. pH of 3.55 is slightly elevated compared to typical Pinot Noir 3.35-3.4 pH but remains within acceptable red grape ranges (3.3-3.5). Titratable acidity of 5.8 g/L is slightly below the recommended 6.0-9.0 g/L range but remains realistic for pre-harvest sampling. All tasting descriptors (pre-harvest, colored, brown, softening, red fruit) are standard viticulture and oenology terminology. The observation log type is appropriate per farmOS documentation. The task data is synthetic but grounded in real agricultural and wine chemistry practices.
